---
title: "References"
---

## Primary method

The spectral–finite-element, time-domain method and its rotational extension:

- @Martinec2000 — the spectral–finite-element approach to 3D viscoelastic
  relaxation in a spherical Earth. **The core method.**
- @MartinecHagedoorn2014 — time-domain rotational feedback.
- @KMM2005 — the sea-level equation with moving shorelines (pseudo-spectral
  fixed point).

## Benchmarks

- @Spada2011 — the GIA-code benchmark (Love numbers, disc-load response, polar
  motion; Charles University benchmark, earth model M3–L70–V01).
- @Martinec2018 — the sea-level-equation intercomparison.

## VILMA physics and coupling

- @Albrecht2024 — coupled PISM–VILMA; documents VILMA's physics.
- @Bagge2021 — VILMA with 3D earth structure.
- @Willeit2022 — the CLIMBER-X Earth system model (the host).

## Supporting theory

- @Farrell1972 — load Love numbers and the deformation of the Earth by surface
  loads.
- @Mitrovica2005 — revised rotational stability theory (fixing $k_f$ from the
  observed flattening).
- @Blewitt2003 — reference frames, geocenter, and degree-1 surface loading.
- @Hanyk1999 — initial-value (time-domain) viscoelastic relaxation.

## Numerical libraries

- @Schaeffer2013 — SHTns, efficient spherical-harmonic transforms.
- @FrigoJohnson2005 — FFTW3.

---

### Full bibliography

::: {#refs}
:::
